title: Create Role and RoleBinding for a ServiceAccount
description: Write a ServiceAccount, Role with pod read access, and RoleBinding connecting them.
starterCode:
# Write three manifests:
# 1. ServiceAccount: monitoring-sa in namespace: monitoring
# 2. Role: pod-reader in namespace: production with rules:
#    - apiGroups: [""] resources: ["pods", "pods/log"] verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
# 3. RoleBinding: monitoring-pod-reader in namespace: production
#    - Binds Role: pod-reader to ServiceAccount monitoring-sa (in namespace: monitoring)

---
# ServiceAccount

---
# Role

---
# RoleBinding
solutionCode:
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: monitoring-sa
  namespace: monitoring
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: pod-reader
  namespace: production
rules:
  - apiGroups: [""]
    resources: ["pods", "pods/log"]
    verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: monitoring-pod-reader
  namespace: production
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: pod-reader
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: monitoring-sa
    namespace: monitoring
hints: Core API resources (pods) use apiGroups: [""] (empty string)
ServiceAccount in subjects needs both name and namespace

validationRules.errorMessage: Use apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1 for RBAC resources

simulatedOutput.success:
serviceaccount/monitoring-sa created
role.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/pod-reader created
rolebinding.rbac.authorization.k8s.io/monitoring-pod-reader created

Verification:
  kubectl auth can-i list pods -n production \
    --as=system:serviceaccount:monitoring:monitoring-sa
  yes

  kubectl auth can-i get secrets -n production \
    --as=system:serviceaccount:monitoring:monitoring-sa
  no
simulatedOutput.failure: RBAC validation failed. Check ServiceAccount, Role rules (apiGroups, resources, verbs), and RoleBinding subjects.
